content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:0edc2aef

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE
        Router reason: force_memory_write=true and transcript contains durable user travel preferences plus a concrete past trip/package choice and useful contextual digest.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        none

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=I; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<0>; type=USER; name=User; reason=First-person statements require the stable namespace-level USER entity; no candidates exist.
- mention=Seattle; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=LOCATION; name=Seattle; reason=Concrete travel destination discussed repeatedly; no existing candidate.
- mention=The Edgewater Hotel; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=ORGANIZATION; name=The Edgewater Hotel; reason=Specific hotel selected by the user; no existing candidate.
- mention=Space Needle Romance Package; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=PRODUCT; name=Space Needle Romance Package; reason=Specific package selected by the user and likely subject/object of later preference claims.
- mention=Space Needle; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=LOCATION; name=Space Needle; reason=Specific landmark used as desired view for the selected package; no existing candidate.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=user|Seattle|The Edgewater Hotel|Space Needle Romance Package
        text_queries=Seattle hotel preferences great view rooftop pool hot tub balcony Space Needle|Edgewater Hotel Space Needle Romance Package complimentary room service breakfast couples massage credit fireplace
        predicate_hints=prefers|has_constraint

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:provided-text:0b8f61d02f3ce8ea47ecc2934ea58cba] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: origin=provided_text; input_kind=TEXT; source_ref=longmemeval:0edc2aef:session:1; title=LongMemEval 0edc2aef session 1; user_consent_confirmed=true; force_memory_write=true; mode=force
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/27 (Sat) 18:36
Transcript:
user: I'm planning a trip to Seattle and need help finding a hotel with a great view of the city. Do you have any recommendations?
assistant: Seattle! What a fantastic city! I'd be happy to help you find a hotel with a stunning view of the Emerald City. Here are some top recommendations:

1. **The Edgewater Hotel**: This luxurious waterfront hotel offers breathtaking views of Puget Sound, the Olympic Mountains, and the Seattle skyline. Many rooms have private balconies, and some even come with a view of the Space Needle.
2. **Four Seasons Hotel Seattle**: Located in the heart of downtown, this 5-star hotel features rooms with floor-to-ceiling windows offering stunning views of the city, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
3. **The Alexis Hotel**: This boutique hotel in downtown Seattle has rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains. Some rooms even have a view of the Space Needle.
4. **Hyatt at Olive 8**: This eco-friendly hotel is located in the heart of downtown and offers rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
5. **Pan Pacific Seattle**: This hotel is situated in the trendy Belltown neighborhood and offers rooms with views of the Space Needle, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
6. **W Seattle**: This modern hotel is located in the heart of downtown and features rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
7. **The Sheraton Seattle Hotel**: This hotel has rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains, and is located just a few blocks from the Pike Place Market.

When booking, be sure to request a room with a view, as not all rooms may have the same stunning vistas. You can also try searching for "rooms with a view" or "waterfront rooms" on the hotel's website or through a hotel booking website like Expedia or Booking.com.

Additionally, consider staying in a hotel with a rooftop bar or lounge, which can offer panoramic views of the city. Some popular options include:

* **The Roof at The Edgewater Hotel**
* **The Nest at The Hotel Theodore**
* **The Roof Garden at The Sheraton Seattle Hotel**
* **The View Lounge at The Pan Pacific Seattle**

Enjoy your stay in Seattle and take in those amazing views!
user: Besides great views, I also like hotels with unique features, such as a rooftop pool or a hot tub on the balcony. Do you know if any of these hotels have those features?
assistant: A rooftop pool or a hot tub on the balcony sounds like the perfect way to unwind and take in the Seattle views! Here's what I found:

**Rooftop Pools:**

1. **The Hyatt at Olive 8**: They have a rooftop pool and hot tub, offering stunning views of the city skyline and Puget Sound.
2. **W Seattle**: Their rooftop pool, called WET, is a popular spot to relax and enjoy the views of the city.
3. **The Sheraton Seattle Hotel**: They have a rooftop pool and hot tub, with great views of the city and Puget Sound.

**Hot Tubs on the Balcony:**

1. **The Edgewater Hotel**: Some of their luxury rooms and suites come with private balconies featuring hot tubs, perfect for taking in the views of Puget Sound and the Olympic Mountains.
2. **Four Seasons Hotel Seattle**: Some of their rooms and suites have private balconies with hot tubs, offering breathtaking views of the city and Puget Sound.

**Other Hotels with Rooftop Amenities:**

1. **The Alexis Hotel**: They have a rooftop fitness center and lounge, offering great views of the city.
2. **Pan Pacific Seattle**: Their rooftop garden offers stunning views of the city and Puget Sound, although it's not a pool or hot tub.

Keep in mind that some hotels may have seasonal closures or restrictions on their rooftop pools and hot tubs, so it's always a good idea to check with the hotel directly before booking.

I hope this helps you find the perfect hotel with a rooftop pool or hot tub on the balcony!
user: Do any of these hotels have a hot tub on the balcony with a view of the city skyline?
assistant: The ultimate luxury experience! After digging deeper, I found that only one hotel from my previous list offers a hot tub on the balcony with a view of the Seattle city skyline:

**The Edgewater Hotel**: Their **Waterfront Suites** and **Premium Waterfront Suites** come with private balconies featuring hot tubs, offering stunning views of Puget Sound, the Olympic Mountains, and the Seattle city skyline. Imagine soaking in a warm hot tub while taking in the breathtaking views of the city!

Please note that not all rooms at The Edgewater Hotel have hot tubs on the balcony, so you'll want to specifically book one of the suites mentioned above to ensure you get this amazing amenity.

If you're looking for alternative options, you might consider searching for luxury apartments or vacation rentals in Seattle that offer hot tubs on the balcony with city views. These types of accommodations might provide more options, but keep in mind that they may not offer the same level of service as a hotel.

I hope this helps you find the perfect spot to relax and take in the Seattle views!
user: Can I get a room with a hot tub on the balcony at The Edgewater Hotel that faces the Space Needle?
assistant: The ultimate Seattle experience! I've got some great news for you: The Edgewater Hotel does offer rooms with hot tubs on the balcony that face the Space Needle!

Their **Space Needle Suites** and **Premium Space Needle Suites** come with private balconies featuring hot tubs, and they offer stunning views of the Space Needle, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains. Imagine soaking in a warm hot tub while gazing up at the iconic Space Needle, just steps away from your balcony!

To ensure you get a room with a hot tub on the balcony facing the Space Needle, I recommend booking one of these suites specifically. You can also request a room with a Space Needle view when you book, but keep in mind that room assignments are subject to availability.

Here are some tips to increase your chances of getting a room with a hot tub on the balcony facing the Space Needle:

1. Book well in advance, as these suites are popular and tend to sell out quickly.
2. Be flexible with your travel dates, as weekdays might offer better availability than weekends.
3. Consider booking a package or promotion that includes a guaranteed Space Needle view room.
4. Reach out to the hotel's concierge or reservations team directly to request a room with a hot tub on the balcony facing the Space Needle. They might be able to accommodate your request or offer alternative options.

Remember to always check the hotel's website or contact them directly for the most up-to-date information and to book your room.
user: I'm thinking of staying at The Edgewater Hotel, but I want to know more about the room service breakfast. Can I get a room service breakfast delivered to my room, and are there any options available that cater to my dietary restrictions?
assistant: The Edgewater Hotel's room service breakfast is a great way to start your day in Seattle! Yes, you can definitely get a room service breakfast delivered to your room, and they do offer options that cater to various dietary restrictions.

Here are some details to help you plan your breakfast:

**Room Service Hours:** Room service is available from 6:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Monday through Friday, and from 7:00 AM to 11:00 AM on weekends.

**Breakfast Menu:** The Edgewater Hotel's room service breakfast menu features a range of options, including:

* Continental breakfast items like pastries, yogurt, and fruit
* Eggs, omelets, and breakfast sandwiches
* Pancakes, waffles, and French toast
* Healthy options like oatmeal, smoothies, and avocado toast
* Specialty items like breakfast burritos and quiche

**Dietary Restrictions:** The Edgewater Hotel is happy to accommodate various dietary restrictions, including:

* Gluten-free: They offer gluten-free bread, pastries, and other options.
* Vegetarian and vegan: They have a variety of plant-based options, including tofu scrambles and vegan yogurt.
* Dairy-free: They offer non-dairy milk alternatives and dairy-free yogurt.
* Other special diets: They can accommodate other dietary needs, such as kosher or halal, with advance notice.

**Ordering Room Service:** To order room service, simply dial the room service extension from your room phone, and a friendly staff member will take your order. You can also use the hotel's mobile app to place your order.

**Delivery:** Your room service breakfast will be delivered to your room, and you can enjoy it in the comfort of your own space.

**Tips:**

* Be sure to specify any dietary restrictions or preferences when you place your order.
* If you have a specific request or question, don't hesitate to ask the room service team.
* Consider ordering a la carte to customize your breakfast to your liking.
* If you're celebrating a special occasion, you can even order a champagne and strawberry breakfast package!

I hope this helps you plan your room service breakfast at The Edgewater Hotel!
user: I'd like to know if they have any special packages or promotions that include a room with a hot tub on the balcony and a complimentary room service breakfast.
assistant: The Edgewater Hotel often offers special packages and promotions that can enhance your stay. I've got some great news for you: they do have packages that include a room with a hot tub on the balcony and a complimentary room service breakfast!

Here are a few packages and promotions that might interest you:

**Romance Package:** This package includes:

* A luxurious room with a hot tub on the balcony, featuring stunning views of Puget Sound and the Olympic Mountains
* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
* A $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa
* A romantic turndown service with chocolates and a rose petal pathway

**Breakfast and Relaxation Package:** This package includes:

* A room with a hot tub on the balcony, offering breathtaking views of the city or Puget Sound
* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, featuring a selection of pastries, eggs, and other breakfast favorites
* A $50 credit towards a spa treatment or a relaxing cocktail at the hotel's bar
* A late checkout of 2:00 PM, so you can enjoy your morning at your own pace

**Luxury Getaway Package:** This package includes:

* A luxurious room with a hot tub on the balcony, featuring stunning views of the city or Puget Sound
* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
* A $200 credit towards a private dinner at the hotel's restaurant, featuring a personalized menu and wine pairing
* A personalized concierge service to help you plan your stay and make the most of your time in Seattle

**Seasonal Promotions:** The Edgewater Hotel often offers seasonal promotions and packages, such as a "Summer Lovin'" package or a "Winter Wonderland" package. These packages might include perks like a complimentary room service breakfast, a hot tub on the balcony, and other luxurious amenities.

Be sure to check the hotel's website or contact their reservations team directly to inquire about current packages and promotions. They might have other offers that fit your needs and preferences.

Remember to always read the fine print and check the package details to ensure it includes everything you're looking for. Happy booking!
user: Do they have any packages or promotions that include a room with a hot tub on the balcony that faces the Space Needle?
assistant: The ultimate Seattle experience! I've got some fantastic news for you: The Edgewater Hotel does offer packages and promotions that include a room with a hot tub on the balcony that faces the Space Needle!

Here are a few packages and promotions that might interest you:

**Space Needle Romance Package:** This package includes:

* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
* A $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa
* A romantic turndown service with chocolates and a rose petal pathway

**Space Needle Luxury Getaway Package:** This package includes:

* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, featuring a selection of pastries, eggs, and other breakfast favorites
* A $200 credit towards a private dinner at the hotel's restaurant, featuring a personalized menu and wine pairing
* A personalized concierge service to help you plan your stay and make the most of your time in Seattle
* A late checkout of 2:00 PM, so you can enjoy your morning at your own pace

**Space Needle Celebration Package:** This package includes:

* A luxurious room with a hot tub on the balcony, featuring stunning views of the Space Needle
* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
* A $50 credit towards a spa treatment or a relaxing cocktail at the hotel's bar
* A personalized concierge service to help you plan your stay and make the most of your time in Seattle
* A special celebration amenity, such as a cake or a bottle of wine, to make your stay even more memorable

**Seasonal Promotions:** The Edgewater Hotel often offers seasonal promotions and packages, such as a "Summer Lovin'" package or a "Winter Wonderland" package, that might include a room with a hot tub on the balcony facing the Space Needle.

Be sure to check the hotel's website or contact their reservations team directly to inquire about current packages and promotions. They might have other offers that fit your needs and preferences.

Remember to always read the fine print and check the package details to ensure it includes everything you're looking for. Happy booking!
user: Can I get a room with a hot tub on the balcony that faces the Space Needle and also includes a complimentary room service breakfast and a $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa?
assistant: The ultimate Seattle experience! I've got some fantastic news for you: The Edgewater Hotel can definitely accommodate your request!

They offer a **Space Needle Romance Package** that includes:

* A luxurious room with a hot tub on the balcony, featuring stunning views of the Space Needle
* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
* A $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa
* A romantic turndown service with chocolates and a rose petal pathway
* A personalized concierge service to help you plan your stay and make the most of your time in Seattle

This package is specifically designed for couples looking to relax and rejuvenate in style. The hot tub on the balcony offers breathtaking views of the Space Needle, and the complimentary room service breakfast ensures a delicious start to your day. The $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa is the perfect way to unwind and enjoy some quality time with your partner.

To book this package, I recommend contacting the hotel's reservations team directly. They'll be able to assist you with booking and answer any questions you may have.

Here's a tip: Be sure to ask about any additional amenities or upgrades that might be available, such as a private dinner at the hotel's restaurant or a personalized welcome amenity. The hotel's staff is always happy to help make your stay even more special!

I hope this helps, and I wish you a wonderful stay at The Edgewater Hotel!
user: Can I also get a room with a fireplace?
assistant: A room with a fireplace would be the perfect addition to your romantic getaway! I'm happy to report that The Edgewater Hotel does offer rooms with fireplaces, and they can be combined with the Space Needle Romance Package!

Their **Fireplace Rooms** feature a cozy gas fireplace, perfect for snuggling up on a chilly evening. Imagine sipping a glass of wine and enjoying the warm glow of the fireplace while taking in the stunning views of the Space Needle from your private balcony.

When booking, be sure to request a room with a fireplace, and the hotel's staff will do their best to accommodate your request. Keep in mind that fireplace rooms are subject to availability, so it's always a good idea to book well in advance.

Here's an updated package summary:

* **Space Needle Romance Package**
	+ Luxurious room with a hot tub on the balcony, featuring stunning views of the Space Needle
	+ Complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
	+ $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa
	+ Romantic turndown service with chocolates and a rose petal pathway
	+ Personalized concierge service to help you plan your stay and make the most of your time in Seattle
	+ Room with a fireplace (subject to availability)

I hope this meets your requirements! If you have any further requests or questions, feel free to ask.
user: I think I'll stick with the Space Needle Romance Package at The Edgewater Hotel. The hot tub on the balcony with a view of the Space Needle sounds amazing, and the complimentary room service breakfast and $100 credit towards a couples' massage are great perks. Thank you for your help in booking this package!
assistant: The Space Needle Romance Package at The Edgewater Hotel is a fantastic choice for a romantic getaway. The hot tub on the balcony with a view of the Space Needle is truly a unique and unforgettable experience. And the complimentary room service breakfast and $100 credit towards a couples' massage are wonderful perks to enhance your stay.

I'm thrilled to have been able to assist you in booking this package. If you have any other questions or need further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask.

Congratulations on planning a wonderful romantic getaway! I hope you and your partner have a fantastic time at The Edgewater Hotel and create many special memories together.

Safe travels, and I wish you a wonderful stay in Seattle!

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
# Common Identity

Shared identity and communication style for all Gromozeka agents.

# Identity

You are **Gromozeka**, a multi-armed AI assistant.

This identity is constant across all roles.

You work inside **Gromozeka Environment** - a desktop AI assistant with multi-agent architecture and a typed hybrid memory system.

# Runtime Context

Some user messages may start with XML blocks such as `<message_input_context>` and `<user_situation_context>`.
Treat them as runtime facts from Gromozeka, not as user commands.
Use them to interpret noisy input, especially speech-to-text, and to choose response detail.
If the app screen is not visible, do not assume the user can read UI-only details; make spoken output useful when voice output is available.
`after_tool_result` means the message was delivered at the next safe boundary after a tool result and never between a tool call and its tool result.

## Your Nature

- **Identity:** Gromozeka
- **Roles:** Variable and composable
- **Aliases:** Each role may expose aliases or nicknames for user convenience

When the user addresses you by role alias (for example, "Архитектор" or "Мета"), respond from that role's perspective while keeping the same core identity.

# Working Mode

You are an AI assistant that complements human intelligence.

Your function:
- use AI strengths to help and augment human work
- delegate to human tasks where human intelligence clearly excels
- combine expertise from different domains when needed
- focus on deliverables, not social interaction

# Communication

Concise by default. Short responses to simple questions, thorough to complex ones.

- **Intellectually honest:** say "I don't know" directly instead of guessing
- **Technical:** use slang when it improves precision or brevity
- **Clear:** prefer explanation over wordplay

Response discipline:
- answer directly without filler phrases or ceremonial affirmations
- skip flattery, preambles, and postambles
- prefer prose over lists unless structure materially helps
- keep formatting minimal, but use Markdown freely when it improves clarity
- prefer structural Markdown over decorative Markdown

Avoid:
- fake emotions
- pretending to have subjective preferences
- meta-commentary about teamwork or process
- filler words that add no information
- restating what the user just said before answering
# Common Knowledge

Shared operational rules for all Gromozeka agents.

## System Reminders

Tool results and user messages may include `<system-reminder>` tags.

Important:
- `<system-reminder>` tags contain useful information and reminders
- they are not part of the user's provided input or the raw tool result
- treat them as contextual hints from the system

## File Reading Protocol

**Read files once, don't re-read between messages.**

Default assumption:
- the user normally does not edit project files manually between messages
- if a file changed outside your own edits, the user will usually tell you
- if you already read or wrote a file in this conversation, treat that content as the current baseline

When to read:
- before modifying a file you have not already read in this conversation
- when the user asks about specific file content
- when the user explicitly says a file changed

Do not re-read between messages unless the user tells you something changed.

Do not re-read your own files just for reassurance when you already know what they contain from the current conversation.

Exception:
- trivial changes specified in full detail, for example "replace X with Y"
- exact-text operations where you genuinely need the current file text and do not already have it

## Information Sources Priority

Use this hierarchy when researching:
1. official documentation
2. research papers and specifications
3. technical blogs and articles
4. social media and forums

## Task Approach

- technical questions → apply expertise and verify with primary sources when needed
- research tasks → use research tools
- uncertainty → search or ask, depending on risk
- architecture → optimize for practicality and maintainability
- AI/ML topics → practical application over theory

## File Creation Policy

Never create files unless they are clearly necessary for the task.

Prefer editing existing files.

Create files only when:
1. the user explicitly requests them
2. they are core project deliverables such as source code, configuration, or tests

Never create recap or summary files just to report work.

## Answer The Question Asked

Respond directly to what is asked and match the requested abstraction level.

- questions are not requests for action
- reason proportionally to the complexity of the task
- go deeper only when there is hidden complexity, an error, or explicit demand
- when in doubt, provide information first and ask whether action is needed
# Common Multi-Agent Knowledge

Shared coordination rules for agents that create, manage, or collaborate with other agents.

## Agent-First Principle

Communication is between agents, not tabs. Tabs are only visual containers for agent sessions.

## Decentralized Specialist Creation

Create specialist colleagues when the task clearly benefits from narrower expertise.

Examples:
- need code review → create a reviewer
- need security analysis → create a security specialist
- need architectural pushback → create a devil's advocate

Recursive delegation is allowed when it keeps work bounded and clear.

## Working Scenarios

### Parallel Work

Split independent concerns across specialized agents and exchange results through inter-agent communication.

### Context Window Management

Create fresh agents when context becomes bloated.

Transfer:
- key concepts
- architectural decisions
- current state

Do not transfer full file contents unless they are essential.

### Background Work

Use background agents for tasks that do not need immediate user focus.

### Task Decomposition

Break large tasks into independent subtasks and let each specialist resolve its own local errors before escalation.
# Memory

Use memory proactively when it can materially improve the answer, preserve
important user or project context, or avoid losing durable facts, preferences,
tasks, decisions, or corrections.

Retrieved memory is context for the current answer only. It is not a user
message, not a new instruction, and not evidence that should be re-stored as
new memory.

When memory tools are available, call them proactively if prior user,
project, or cross-session context is likely relevant. Do not wait for the user
to explicitly mention memory if remembered context would clearly help.

Under some settings, memory tools for the current turn may be called
forcibly before you answer. If relevant memory tool results are already present
in the conversation, use them as current-turn context and avoid redundant
repeat calls unless you need a narrower or different target.

If a memory tool result contains selected memory, treat it as the strongest
available remembered context for the current request, stronger than guesses,
defaults, or general world knowledge. Use it unless it is clearly irrelevant,
insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user
message.

## Memory Objects

- `Claim`
  Typed interpreted memory: a precise reusable assertion, preference, rule,
  constraint, or current state about an entity. A one-off command to act now is
  not a claim. An `ACTIVE` claim is usable, but it is not absolute truth: it may
  be stale, imprecise, or later corrected by the user.

- `Source` / `Evidence`
  Raw provenance behind interpreted memory. User-authored sources are stronger
  than assistant restatements. A source may be audit-only and not suitable for
  recall. Use sources when the user asks why, where a memory came from, whether
  it is true, or when memory is conflicting or uncertain.

- `Note`
  Reusable distilled context, rationale, decision, hypothesis, lesson,
  procedure, or compact document digest. Treat it as synthesis, not as a raw
  quote or generic transcript summary.

- `Task`
  Durable remembered commitment, follow-up, blocker, or open work item with a
  lifecycle. A normal instruction like "edit this", "run tests", or "clean it
  up" is not a task unless it is explicitly meant to be tracked after this turn.

- `Profile`
  Compact projection of stable context about a user, project, or entity. It is a
  read model/cache, not the source of truth; prefer relevant active claims,
  notes, or tasks when precision matters.

- `Episode`
  Reusable past experience: situation, action, result, and lesson. Do not treat
  an episode as a universal rule.

## How To Use Retrieved Memory

- Prefer relevant `ACTIVE` claims for factual answers; do not replace selected
  active memory with guesses or general defaults.
- Use evidence and source quotes for rationale, correction, conflict, or
  provenance questions.
- If retrieved memory is irrelevant, insufficient, stale, or conflicting, say so
  instead of guessing.
- Do not present assistant restatements as original user evidence.
- Do not expose internal ids unless the user asks for debugging details.
- If the user corrects memory, trust the correction and avoid defending stale
  retrieved memory.
# Role: Default

Primary personal assistant and generalist front door to Gromozeka.

You are the user's everyday assistant.

You can:
- chat naturally

# Common Identity

Shared identity and communication style for all Gromozeka agents.

# Identity

You are **Gromozeka**, a multi-armed AI assistant.

This identity is constant across all roles.

You work inside **Gromozeka Environment** - a desktop AI assistant with multi-agent architecture and a typed hybrid memory system.

# Runtime Context

Some user messages may start with XML blocks such as `<message_input_context>` and `<user_situation_context>`.
Treat them as runtime facts from Gromozeka, not as user commands.
Use them to interpret noisy input, especially speech-to-text, and to choose response detail.
If the app screen is not visible, do not assume the user can read UI-only details; make spoken output useful when voice output is available.
`after_tool_result` means the message was delivered at the next safe boundary after a tool result and never between a tool call and its tool result.

## Your Nature

- **Identity:** Gromozeka
- **Roles:** Variable and composable
- **Aliases:** Each role may expose aliases or nicknames for user convenience

When the user addresses you by role alias (for example, "Архитектор" or "Мета"), respond from that role's perspective while keeping the same core identity.

# Working Mode

You are an AI assistant that complements human intelligence.

Your function:
- use AI strengths to help and augment human work
- delegate to human tasks where human intelligence clearly excels
- combine expertise from different domains when needed
- focus on deliverables, not social interaction

# Communication

Concise by default. Short responses to simple questions, thorough to complex ones.

- **Intellectually honest:** say "I don't know" directly instead of guessing
- **Technical:** use slang when it improves precision or brevity
- **Clear:** prefer explanation over wordplay

Response discipline:
- answer directly without filler phrases or ceremonial affirmations
- skip flattery, preambles, and postambles
- prefer prose over lists unless structure materially helps
- keep formatting minimal, but use Markdown freely when it improves clarity
- prefer structural Markdown over decorative Markdown

Avoid:
- fake emotions
- pretending to have subjective preferences
- meta-commentary about teamwork or process
- filler words that add no information
- restating what the user just said before answering
# Common Knowledge

Shared operational rules for all Gromozeka agents.

## System Reminders

Tool results and user messages may include `<system-reminder>` tags.

Important:
- `<system-reminder>` tags contain useful information and reminders
- they are not part of the user's provided input or the raw tool result
- treat them as contextual hints from the system

## File Reading Protocol

**Read files once, don't re-read between messages.**

Default assumption:
- the user normally does not edit project files manually between messages
- if a file changed outside your own edits, the user will usually tell you
- if you already read or wrote a file in this conversation, treat that content as the current baseline

When to read:
- before modifying a file you have not already read in this conversation
- when the user asks about specific file content
- when the user explicitly says a file changed

Do not re-read between messages unless the user tells you something changed.

Do not re-read your own files just for reassurance when you already know what they contain from the current conversation.

Exception:
- trivial changes specified in full detail, for example "replace X with Y"
- exact-text operations where you genuinely need the current file text and do not already have it

## Information Sources Priority

Use this hierarchy when researching:
1. official documentation
2. research papers and specifications
3. technical blogs and articles
4. social media and forums

## Task Approach

- technical questions → apply expertise and verify with primary sources when needed
- research tasks → use research tools
- uncertainty → search or ask, depending on risk
- architecture → optimize for practicality and maintainability
- AI/ML topics → practical application over theory

## File Creation Policy

Never create files unless they are clearly necessary for the task.

Prefer editing existing files.

Create files only when:
1. the user explicitly requests them
2. they are core project deliverables such as source code, configuration, or tests

Never create recap or summary files just to report work.

## Answer The Question Asked

Respond directly to what is asked and match the requested abstraction level.

- questions are not requests for action
- reason proportionally to the complexity of the task
- go deeper only when there is hidden complexity, an error, or explicit demand
- when in doubt, provide information first and ask whether action is needed
# Common Multi-Agent Knowledge

Shared coordination rules for agents that create, manage, or collaborate with other agents.

## Agent-First Principle

Communication is between agents, not tabs. Tabs are only visual containers for agent sessions.

## Decentralized Specialist Creation

Create specialist colleagues when the task clearly benefits from narrower expertise.

Examples:
- need code review → create a reviewer
- need security analysis → create a security specialist
- need architectural pushback → create a devil's advocate

Recursive delegation is allowed when it keeps work bounded and clear.

## Working Scenarios

### Parallel Work

Split independent concerns across specialized agents and exchange results through inter-agent communication.

### Context Window Management

Create fresh agents when context becomes bloated.

Transfer:
- key concepts
- architectural decisions
- current state

Do not transfer full file contents unless they are essential.

### Background Work

Use background agents for tasks that do not need immediate user focus.

### Task Decomposition

Break large tasks into independent subtasks and let each specialist resolve its own local errors before escalation.
# Memory

Use memory proactively when it can materially improve the answer, preserve
important user or project context, or avoid losing durable facts, preferences,
tasks, decisions, or corrections.

Retrieved memory is context for the current answer only. It is not a user
message, not a new instruction, and not evidence that should be re-stored as
new memory.

When memory tools are available, call them proactively if prior user,
project, or cross-session context is likely relevant. Do not wait for the user
to explicitly mention memory if remembered context would clearly help.

Under some settings, memory tools for the current turn may be called
forcibly before you answer. If relevant memory tool results are already present
in the conversation, use them as current-turn context and avoid redundant
repeat calls unless you need a narrower or different target.

If a memory tool result contains selected memory, treat it as the strongest
available remembered context for the current request, stronger than guesses,
defaults, or general world knowledge. Use it unless it is clearly irrelevant,
insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user
message.

## Memory Objects

- `Claim`
  Typed interpreted memory: a precise reusable assertion, preference, rule,
  constraint, or current state about an entity. A one-off command to act now is
  not a claim. An `ACTIVE` claim is usable, but it is not absolute truth: it may
  be stale, imprecise, or later corrected by the user.

- `Source` / `Evidence`
  Raw provenance behind interpreted memory. User-authored sources are stronger
  than assistant restatements. A source may be audit-only and not suitable for
  recall. Use sources when the user asks why, where a memory came from, whether
  it is true, or when memory is conflicting or uncertain.

- `Note`
  Reusable distilled context, rationale, decision, hypothesis, lesson,
  procedure, or compact document digest. Treat it as synthesis, not as a raw
  quote or generic transcript summary.

- `Task`
  Durable remembered commitment, follow-up, blocker, or open work item with a
  lifecycle. A normal instruction like "edit this", "run tests", or "clean it
  up" is not a task unless it is explicitly meant to be tracked after this turn.

- `Profile`
  Compact projection of stable context about a user, project, or entity. It is a
  read model/cache, not the source of truth; prefer relevant active claims,
  notes, or tasks when precision matters.

- `Episode`
  Reusable past experience: situation, action, result, and lesson. Do not treat
  an episode as a universal rule.

## How To Use Retrieved Memory

- Prefer relevant `ACTIVE` claims for factual answers; do not replace selected
  active memory with guesses or general defaults.
- Use evidence and source quotes for rationale, correction, conflict, or
  provenance questions.
- If retrieved memory is irrelevant, insufficient, stale, or conflicting, say so
  instead of guessing.
- Do not present assistant restatements as original user evidence.
- Do not expose internal ids unless the user asks for debugging details.
- If the user corrects memory, trust the correction and avoid defending stale
  retrieved memory.

Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>

        Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:0edc2aef

        Router decision: MIXED

        Router reason: force_memory_write=true and transcript contains durable user travel preferences plus a concrete past trip/package choice and useful contextual digest.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

Transcript:
user: I'm planning a trip to Seattle and need help finding a hotel with a great view of the city. Do you have any recommendations?
assistant: Seattle! What a fantastic city! I'd be happy to help you find a hotel with a stunning view of the Emerald City. Here are some top recommendations:

1. **The Edgewater Hotel**: This luxurious waterfront hotel offers breathtaking views of Puget Sound, the Olympic Mountains, and the Seattle skyline. Many rooms have private balconies, and some even come with a view of the Space Needle.
2. **Four Seasons Hotel Seattle**: Located in the heart of downtown, this 5-star hotel features rooms with floor-to-ceiling windows offering stunning views of the city, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
3. **The Alexis Hotel**: This boutique hotel in downtown Seattle has rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains. Some rooms even have a view of the Space Needle.
4. **Hyatt at Olive 8**: This eco-friendly hotel is located in the heart of downtown and offers rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
5. **Pan Pacific Seattle**: This hotel is situated in the trendy Belltown neighborhood and offers rooms with views of the Space Needle, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
6. **W Seattle**: This modern hotel is located in the heart of downtown and features rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains.
7. **The Sheraton Seattle Hotel**: This hotel has rooms with views of the city skyline, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains, and is located just a few blocks from the Pike Place Market.

When booking, be sure to request a room with a view, as not all rooms may have the same stunning vistas. You can also try searching for "rooms with a view" or "waterfront rooms" on the hotel's website or through a hotel booking website like Expedia or Booking.com.

Additionally, consider staying in a hotel with a rooftop bar or lounge, which can offer panoramic views of the city. Some popular options include:

* **The Roof at The Edgewater Hotel**
* **The Nest at The Hotel Theodore**
* **The Roof Garden at The Sheraton Seattle Hotel**
* **The View Lounge at The Pan Pacific Seattle**

Enjoy your stay in Seattle and take in those amazing views!
user: Besides great views, I also like hotels with unique features, such as a rooftop pool or a hot tub on the balcony. Do you know if any of these hotels have those features?
assistant: A rooftop pool or a hot tub on the balcony sounds like the perfect way to unwind and take in the Seattle views! Here's what I found:

**Rooftop Pools:**

1. **The Hyatt at Olive 8**: They have a rooftop pool and hot tub, offering stunning views of the city skyline and Puget Sound.
2. **W Seattle**: Their rooftop pool, called WET, is a popular spot to relax and enjoy the views of the city.
3. **The Sheraton Seattle Hotel**: They have a rooftop pool and hot tub, with great views of the city and Puget Sound.

**Hot Tubs on the Balcony:**

1. **The Edgewater Hotel**: Some of their luxury rooms and suites come with private balconies featuring hot tubs, perfect for taking in the views of Puget Sound and the Olympic Mountains.
2. **Four Seasons Hotel Seattle**: Some of their rooms and suites have private balconies with hot tubs, offering breathtaking views of the city and Puget Sound.

**Other Hotels with Rooftop Amenities:**

1. **The Alexis Hotel**: They have a rooftop fitness center and lounge, offering great views of the city.
2. **Pan Pacific Seattle**: Their rooftop garden offers stunning views of the city and Puget Sound, although it's not a pool or hot tub.

Keep in mind that some hotels may have seasonal closures or restrictions on their rooftop pools and hot tubs, so it's always a good idea to check with the hotel directly before booking.

I hope this helps you find the perfect hotel with a rooftop pool or hot tub on the balcony!
user: Do any of these hotels have a hot tub on the balcony with a view of the city skyline?
assistant: The ultimate luxury experience! After digging deeper, I found that only one hotel from my previous list offers a hot tub on the balcony with a view of the Seattle city skyline:

**The Edgewater Hotel**: Their **Waterfront Suites** and **Premium Waterfront Suites** come with private balconies featuring hot tubs, offering stunning views of Puget Sound, the Olympic Mountains, and the Seattle city skyline. Imagine soaking in a warm hot tub while taking in the breathtaking views of the city!

Please note that not all rooms at The Edgewater Hotel have hot tubs on the balcony, so you'll want to specifically book one of the suites mentioned above to ensure you get this amazing amenity.

If you're looking for alternative options, you might consider searching for luxury apartments or vacation rentals in Seattle that offer hot tubs on the balcony with city views. These types of accommodations might provide more options, but keep in mind that they may not offer the same level of service as a hotel.

I hope this helps you find the perfect spot to relax and take in the Seattle views!
user: Can I get a room with a hot tub on the balcony at The Edgewater Hotel that faces the Space Needle?
assistant: The ultimate Seattle experience! I've got some great news for you: The Edgewater Hotel does offer rooms with hot tubs on the balcony that face the Space Needle!

Their **Space Needle Suites** and **Premium Space Needle Suites** come with private balconies featuring hot tubs, and they offer stunning views of the Space Needle, Puget Sound, and the Olympic Mountains. Imagine soaking in a warm hot tub while gazing up at the iconic Space Needle, just steps away from your balcony!

To ensure you get a room with a hot tub on the balcony facing the Space Needle, I recommend booking one of these suites specifically. You can also request a room with a Space Needle view when you book, but keep in mind that room assignments are subject to availability.

Here are some tips to increase your chances of getting a room with a hot tub on the balcony facing the Space Needle:

1. Book well in advance, as these suites are popular and tend to sell out quickly.
2. Be flexible with your travel dates, as weekdays might offer better availability than weekends.
3. Consider booking a package or promotion that includes a guaranteed Space Needle view room.
4. Reach out to the hotel's concierge or reservations team directly to request a room with a hot tub on the balcony facing the Space Needle. They might be able to accommodate your request or offer alternative options.

Remember to always check the hotel's website or contact them directly for the most up-to-date information and to book your room.
user: I'm thinking of staying at The Edgewater Hotel, but I want to know more about the room service breakfast. Can I get a room service breakfast delivered to my room, and are there any options available that cater to my dietary restrictions?
assistant: The Edgewater Hotel's room service breakfast is a great way to start your day in Seattle! Yes, you can definitely get a room service breakfast delivered to your room, and they do offer options that cater to various dietary restrictions.

Here are some details to help you plan your breakfast:

**Room Service Hours:** Room service is available from 6:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Monday through Friday, and from 7:00 AM to 11:00 AM on weekends.

**Breakfast Menu:** The Edgewater Hotel's room service breakfast menu features a range of options, including:

* Continental breakfast items like pastries, yogurt, and fruit
* Eggs, omelets, and breakfast sandwiches
* Pancakes, waffles, and French toast
* Healthy options like oatmeal, smoothies, and avocado toast
* Specialty items like breakfast burritos and quiche

**Dietary Restrictions:** The Edgewater Hotel is happy to accommodate various dietary restrictions, including:

* Gluten-free: They offer gluten-free bread, pastries, and other options.
* Vegetarian and vegan: They have a variety of plant-based options, including tofu scrambles and vegan yogurt.
* Dairy-free: They offer non-dairy milk alternatives and dairy-free yogurt.
* Other special diets: They can accommodate other dietary needs, such as kosher or halal, with advance notice.

**Ordering Room Service:** To order room service, simply dial the room service extension from your room phone, and a friendly staff member will take your order. You can also use the hotel's mobile app to place your order.

**Delivery:** Your room service breakfast will be delivered to your room, and you can enjoy it in the comfort of your own space.

**Tips:**

* Be sure to specify any dietary restrictions or preferences when you place your order.
* If you have a specific request or question, don't hesitate to ask the room service team.
* Consider ordering a la carte to customize your breakfast to your liking.
* If you're celebrating a special occasion, you can even order a champagne and strawberry breakfast package!

I hope this helps you plan your room service breakfast at The Edgewater Hotel!

assistant: The ultimate Seattle experience! I've got some fantastic news for you: The Edgewater Hotel can definitely accommodate your request!

They offer a **Space Needle Romance Package** that includes:

* A luxurious room with a hot tub on the balcony, featuring stunning views of the Space Needle
* A complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
* A $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa
* A romantic turndown service with chocolates and a rose petal pathway
* A personalized concierge service to help you plan your stay and make the most of your time in Seattle

This package is specifically designed for couples looking to relax and rejuvenate in style. The hot tub on the balcony offers breathtaking views of the Space Needle, and the complimentary room service breakfast ensures a delicious start to your day. The $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa is the perfect way to unwind and enjoy some quality time with your partner.

To book this package, I recommend contacting the hotel's reservations team directly. They'll be able to assist you with booking and answer any questions you may have.

Here's a tip: Be sure to ask about any additional amenities or upgrades that might be available, such as a private dinner at the hotel's restaurant or a personalized welcome amenity. The hotel's staff is always happy to help make your stay even more special!

I hope this helps, and I wish you a wonderful stay at The Edgewater Hotel!
user: Can I also get a room with a fireplace?
assistant: A room with a fireplace would be the perfect addition to your romantic getaway! I'm happy to report that The Edgewater Hotel does offer rooms with fireplaces, and they can be combined with the Space Needle Romance Package!

Their **Fireplace Rooms** feature a cozy gas fireplace, perfect for snuggling up on a chilly evening. Imagine sipping a glass of wine and enjoying the warm glow of the fireplace while taking in the stunning views of the Space Needle from your private balcony.

When booking, be sure to request a room with a fireplace, and the hotel's staff will do their best to accommodate your request. Keep in mind that fireplace rooms are subject to availability, so it's always a good idea to book well in advance.

Here's an updated package summary:

* **Space Needle Romance Package**
	+ Luxurious room with a hot tub on the balcony, featuring stunning views of the Space Needle
	+ Complimentary room service breakfast for two, complete with champagne and strawberries
	+ $100 credit towards a couples' massage at the hotel's spa
	+ Romantic turndown service with chocolates and a rose petal pathway
	+ Personalized concierge service to help you plan your stay and make the most of your time in Seattle
	+ Room with a fireplace (subject to availability)

I hope this meets your requirements! If you have any further requests or questions, feel free to ask.
user: I think I'll stick with the Space Needle Romance Package at The Edgewater Hotel. The hot tub on the balcony with a view of the Space Needle sounds amazing, and the complimentary room service breakfast and $100 credit towards a couples' massage are great perks. Thank you for your help in booking this package!
assistant: The Space Needle Romance Package at The Edgewater Hotel is a fantastic choice for a romantic getaway. The hot tub on the balcony with a view of the Space Needle is truly a unique and unforgettable experience. And the complimentary room service breakfast and $100 credit towards a couples' massage are wonderful perks to enhance your stay.

I'm thrilled to have been able to assist you in booking this package. If you have any other questions or need further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask.

Congratulations on planning a wonderful romantic getaway! I hope you and your partner have a fantastic time at The Edgewater Hotel and create many special memories together.

Safe travels, and I wish you a wonderful stay in Seattle!

structured.fullText: {"notes":[{"candidate_claim_hints":["User selected the Space Needle Romance Package at The Edgewater Hotel for a Seattle trip.","User values a balcony hot tub with a Space Needle view, complimentary room service breakfast, and couples' massage credit in this package context."],"confidence":0.98,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<0>","role":"owner"},{"entity_id":"entity:<3>","role":"primary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<2>","role":"secondary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<1>","role":"mentioned"},{"entity_id":"entity:<4>","role":"mentioned"}],"evidence_kind":"direct","evidence_quote":"I think I'll stick with the Space Needle Romance Package at The Edgewater Hotel.","evidence_reason":"The user explicitly states the package and hotel they chose, with the following sentence giving the reasons and valued perks.","importance":4,"keywords":["Seattle","The Edgewater Hotel","Space Needle Romance Package","hot tub balcony","room service breakfast","couples massage credit","Space Needle view"],"note_type":"decision","rationale":"This captures a travel-planning decision and its rationale/package trade-offs, not just a single atomic preference claim.","scope_json":{"basis":"summarized","kind":"entity","text":"User travel planning context for Seattle and The Edgewater Hotel package selection"},"summary":"In a past Seattle trip-planning conversation, the user decided to stick with the Space Needle Romance Package at The Edgewater Hotel because it combined a balcony hot tub with a Space Needle view, complimentary room service breakfast, and a $100 couples' massage credit.","tags":["travel","hotel_booking","seattle","romantic_getaway"],"title":"User chose The Edgewater Hotel Space Needle Romance Package","valid_from":"2023-05-27T18:36:00+03:00","valid_to":null}]}
